rulings.Case Title: PORTFOLIO RECOVERY ASSOCIATES, LLC VS. CATHY L FREEMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enter Judgment Pursuant To Terms Of Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 20, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF PORTFOLIO RECOVERY ASSOCIATES, LLC'S Motion To Enter Judgment Pursuant To Terms Of Settlement Agreement. Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: SHEILA SCOTT VS. CYPRESS SECURITY, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Litigation
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 20, 2015, Line 4, DEFENDANT CYPRESS SECURITY, LLC'S Hearing On Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Litigation. The motion is denied. Defendant waived the right to compel arbitration. Defendant unreasonably delayed bringing this motion, the parties have already exchanged discovery requests, and defendant was already deposed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 20, 2015, Line 6, PLAINTIFF HUDSON MARR'S Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Settlement. Continued to July 27, 2015 to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than July 20, 2015 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). rulings.Case Title: ESTATE OF DORIS FOSTER VS. HEBREW HOME FOR THE AGED DISABLED ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 20, 2015, Line 9, DEFENDANT HEBREW HOME FOR THE AGED DISABLED'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with 10 days leave to amend. A complaint must allege the facts giving rise to an Elder Abuse cause of action with particularity. (Carter v. Healthcare Paradise Valley LLC (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 396, 407, "the facts constituting the neglect and establishing the causal link between the neglect and the injury must be pleaded with particularity, in accordance with the pleading rules governing statutory claims.") Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: WHITNEY CLARKE VS. LUX LEASING, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 20, 2015, Line 11, DEFENDANT LOOP TRANSPORTATION, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The court overrules the demurrer. The City is not a necessary party and CC 3482 does not apply to this case. Plaintiff is not seeking to enjoin the pilot program or attacking the city ordinance. Rather, plaintiff filed this action because defendant is not complying with the ordinance and/or its guidelines. (Pars. 4-5 of the Complaint). The complaint sufficiently alleges UCL standing. Par. 6 of the Complaint alleges that defendant's conduct has deprived plaintiffs' of the right to quiet enjoyment of their homes. Such conduct could lead to the diminution in value of plaintiffs' leaseholds. At most, defendant raises a factual issue regarding standing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: WHITNEY CLARKE VS. LUX LEASING, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 20, 2015, Line 11, DEFENDANT LOOP TRANSPORTATION, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. The court denies the motion to strike and denies plaintiffs' request for sanctions. Defendant fails to show that the requests for injunctive relief are improper as a matter of law. The trial judge determining a preliminary or permanent injunction is better situated to determine the precise scope of any injunction that is issued. The court denies the motion as to the "socioeconomic" allegations. They provide background to the case and are not clearly irrelevant. They also relate to the benefit and burden of defendant's conduct, which is relevant to the nuisance determination. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)
